title: Nonea lutea
uses: Nonea lutea is known to have various medicinal properties. It has been used traditionally in folk medicine to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. taxonomy: Nonea lutea is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a part of the genus Nonea, which is a group of plants native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Nonea lutea is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Boraginales, Family: Boraginaceae, Genus: Nonea, Species: N. lutea. The Boraginaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. These plants are known for their distinctive bell-shaped flowers and hairy leaves.

intro: Nonea lutea, also known as yellow nonea or nonea, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Boraginaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing relief from various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Nonea lutea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Nonea lutea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and creams, which can be applied topically to treat skin conditions or taken internally to alleviate respiratory issues.
